title: Documents Object
description: The Documents object provides access to all of the currently open documents and provides methods to create and open documents.

methods.name: add
methods.description: Creates and opens a new document of the specified type.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: item
methods.description: Function that returns the specified document using an index into the collection.

methods.name: open
methods.description: Opens an item that has previously been saved.

properties.name: count
properties.description: Returns the number of currently open documents. This includes documents that are visible and invisible.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

related_samples.name: Loft Feature API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new loft feature.

add.title: Documents.add Method
add.description: Creates and opens a new document of the specified type. Remarks

syntax.python:
# Uses no optional arguments.
returnValue = documents_var.add(documentType)

# Uses optional arguments.
returnValue = documents_var.add(documentType, visible, options)

parameters.name: documentType
parameters.description: A value from the DocumentTypes enum that specifies the type of document to create.

parameters.name: visible
parameters.description: Optional argument specifying is the document should be visible or not. Currently, documents can only be created visibly so this argument must always be true.   This is an optional argument whose default value is True.

parameters.name: options
parameters.description: Various options that are supported that are specific to the document type. See the documentation for the DocumentTypes enum for information about the options supported for the various types.   This is an optional argument whose default value is null.

related_samples.name: Loft Feature API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new loft feature.

classType.title: Documents.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

item.title: Documents.item Method
item.description: Function that returns the specified document using an index into the collection.
item.return_description: Returns the specified item or null if an invalid index was specified.

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: The index of the item within the collection to return. The first item in the collection has an index of 0.

open.title: Documents.open Method
open.description: Opens an item that has previously been saved.

syntax.python:
# Uses no optional arguments.
returnValue = documents_var.open(dataFile)

# Uses optional arguments.
returnValue = documents_var.open(dataFile, visible)

open.return_description: Returns the open document or null if the open failed.

parameters.name: visible
parameters.description: Specifies if the document should be opened visibly or not.   This is an optional argument whose default value is True.

count.title: Documents.count Property
count.description: Returns the number of currently open documents. This includes documents that are visible and invisible.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = documents_var.count

isValid.title: Documents.isValid Property
isValid.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = documents_var.isValid

objectType.title: Documents.objectType Property
objectType.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object. It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = documents_var.objectType
